content.text: Communication during an incident is as important as the technical response. A clear update to stakeholders preserves trust; a garbled or absent one creates a second incident on top of the first. This topic defines who to tell, what to say, when to say it, and how — for both internal and external audiences — across the full lifecycle of a GenAI production incident.

attrs.alt: Incident communications flow diagram showing IC and comms liaison routing messages to internal engineering and leadership and external status page and enterprise customers, with message types by phase

content.text: Three principles govern every message you send during an incident:
— only the IC or a designated Comms Liaison speaks externally. No responder should post customer-facing updates without explicit IC approval.
— state what you know, what you don't know, and what you're doing to find out. Never include an unconfirmed root cause in an external message.
— a brief 'we are investigating' update 10 minutes in is better than a polished message 40 minutes late. Silence breeds customer support tickets.
These principles apply to internal comms too. Engineering updates should state facts and actions, not theories and blame.
Post this to the engineering Slack channel immediately after opening the incident channel:

:rotating_light: SEV1 INCIDENT DECLARED — INC-2024-03-15-042

Impact: ~40% of inference API requests returning 503 in us-east-1a. P99 latency > 30s.
Started: 14:18 UTC (approx)
IC: @alice | Bridge: <zoom-link> | Channel: #inc-2024-03-15-042

We are investigating. Next update in 30 minutes or when we have new information.
The first public status page update is posted no later than 30 minutes after declaration for SEV1. Use plain language — customers read this, not engineers:

Investigating — Inference API Latency

We are investigating reports of elevated latency and errors affecting the Inference API. Some requests may be timing out or returning errors. Our team is actively working to identify the cause and restore full service. We will provide an update within 30 minutes.
in this message: no root cause, no blame, no internal system names, no promises about time to resolution.
Every 10 minutes on the incident channel (internal); every 30 minutes on the status page (external). Use the same structure each time to make updates scannable:

--- INCIDENT UPDATE 14:35 UTC ---
Status: INVESTIGATING
Hypothesis: OOM on inference pods in us-east-1a (medium confidence)
User impact: ~40% of requests in us-east-1a affected
Actions in flight:
  - Dave: Cordoning us-east-1a to isolate blast radius (ETA 14:40)
  - Eve: Reviewing Helm memory config (ETA 14:40)
Next update: 14:45 UTC or when mitigation is deployed
--- END UPDATE ---
The external status page version strips the internal action owners and hypothesis confidence and focuses on user impact:

Update — We have identified the affected component and are actively implementing a fix.
Requests affected: approximately 40% in the us-east-1a region.
We are working to restore service and will provide the next update by 15:00 UTC.
When a mitigation is deployed and impact begins to reduce, send a mitigation message before declaring resolution. This manages customer expectations during the recovery window:

Monitoring — Inference API Latency

We have deployed a mitigation that is reducing the impact on the Inference API. Latency is improving and the error rate is declining. We are monitoring to confirm full recovery before declaring this incident resolved. A final update will follow once the service is fully restored.

--- INCIDENT RESOLVED 14:57 UTC ---
INC-2024-03-15-042 | Duration: 39 minutes

Resolution: Cordoned us-east-1a and rerouted traffic to us-west-2.
Root cause (preliminary): Memory limit misconfiguration in Helm values (4Gi configured, pods allocated 8Gi).
User impact: ~40% of inference requests in us-east-1a experienced elevated latency or errors.
Max P99 observed: 34.2s

Post-mortem: 2024-03-16 10:00 UTC — @alice to schedule
Action items: Helm config audit by @dave (due 2024-03-22)

Thank you all for the fast response.
--- END ---

Resolved — Inference API Latency

The Inference API has been fully restored as of 14:57 UTC. The incident lasted approximately 39 minutes and affected a subset of requests in the us-east-1a region. We apologise for the disruption. A post-incident report will be published within five business days with a full root cause analysis and preventive actions.
Enterprise customers with dedicated Slack channels or SLAs for uptime notification require a personalised communication — not a generic status page link.

To: {customer_name} Technical Contact <{customer_email}>
From: incidents@yourcompany.com
Subject: [Action Required] Service Disruption — {yourcompany} Inference API — {date}

Dear {customer_name} team,

We are writing to notify you of an ongoing incident affecting the {yourcompany} Inference API.

Impact: Elevated latency and errors on inference requests
Start time: {start_time} UTC
Affected region: us-east-1a
Your estimated impact: Based on your recent usage patterns, some of your requests
may have been affected during this window.

What we are doing: Our engineering team is actively working to restore full service.
We have already implemented an initial mitigation and are monitoring recovery.

Next update: We will email you again by {next_update_time} UTC with a full status update,
or immediately when the incident is resolved.

You can track live updates at: https://status.yourcompany.com

We apologise for the impact on your service.

— {yourcompany} Incident Team
  On behalf of IC: {ic_name}

from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Optional
import smtplib
from email.mime.text import MIMEText

@dataclass
class IncidentNotification:
    incident_id: str
    severity: str
    title: str
    start_time: str
    affected_regions: list[str]
    ic_name: str
    next_update_time: Optional[str] = None

def notify_enterprise_customers(
    notification: IncidentNotification,
    customers: list[dict],
    smtp_client: smtplib.SMTP,
) -> list[str]:
    """Send personalised notifications to affected enterprise customers."""
    notified = []
    for customer in customers:
        if not _is_customer_affected(customer, notification.affected_regions):
            continue
        body = _render_email_template(notification, customer)
        msg = MIMEText(body)
        msg["Subject"] = (
            f"[{notification.severity}] Service Disruption — "
            f"Inference API — {notification.start_time[:10]}"
        )
        msg["From"] = "incidents@yourcompany.com"
        msg["To"] = customer["contact_email"]
        smtp_client.send_message(msg)
        notified.append(customer["name"])
    return notified

def _is_customer_affected(customer: dict, affected_regions: list[str]) -> bool:
    customer_regions = set(customer.get("primary_regions", []))
    return bool(customer_regions & set(affected_regions))

A status page is the primary external communication channel during an incident. It must be updated on a predictable cadence so customers know when to expect the next update and don't flood support channels.
Safety incidents — harmful outputs, PII leakage, data breach, unauthorized access — require a different communications protocol. The key constraint:
no external communication without Legal review.
Declare SEV1 SAFETY, page IC and Legal simultaneously
IC briefs Legal on scope: what data, how many users, what system
Legal determines notification obligations (GDPR, CCPA, HIPAA)
IC and Legal draft customer notification (reviewed before send)
Data Protection Authority notification (GDPR Art.33 requires within 72h)
Post-mortem with Legal, Security, and affected customer representatives
For GDPR-regulated data breaches, the 72-hour clock starts from the moment the controller
of the breach — not from when it is confirmed. When in doubt, notify.
Legal Notification Hold — What Not to Do
Do not post any status page update mentioning 'data' or 'user data' before Legal review
Do not confirm or deny a breach to enterprise customers before Legal approves the language
Do not discuss the incident on public social media or in engineering blog posts during the legal review window
Do not delete logs or artifacts that may be required for regulatory reporting
What Not to Say: Common Comms Mistakes
"Likely caused by a bug introduced in yesterday's deploy"
If missed, trust damage is compounded
"The OOM in k8s pods is causing us-east-1a to fall behind on inference"
Customers don't know what this means
"The Inference API is experiencing elevated latency in one region."
"AWS is having network issues causing this"
Shifts blame but doesn't help customers
"We are working with our infrastructure providers to resolve this."
Customers assume the worst and open support tickets
Post a 'still investigating' message on schedule even with no new info
Missing a scheduled status update because the team is heads-down debugging is a comms anti-pattern. A bot can send reminder pings to the comms liaison on schedule:

import asyncio
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

async def run_comms_cadence(
    slack_client,
    comms_liaison_id: str,
    channel_id: str,
    severity: str,
) -> None:
    """Remind comms liaison to post updates on schedule."""
    interval_minutes = 10 if severity == "SEV1" else 15
    status_page_interval = 30  # always 30 min for external
    last_internal = datetime.utcnow()
    last_external = datetime.utcnow()

    while True:
        await asyncio.sleep(60)  # check every minute
        now = datetime.utcnow()

        if (now - last_internal).seconds >= interval_minutes * 60:
            await slack_client.chat_postMessage(
                channel=channel_id,
                text=(
                    f":bell: <@{comms_liaison_id}> — internal update due "
                    f"(last posted {interval_minutes} min ago). "
                    f"Post current status to <#{channel_id}>."
                )
            )
            last_internal = now

        if (now - last_external).seconds >= status_page_interval * 60:
            await slack_client.chat_postMessage(
                channel=channel_id,
                text=(
                    f":globe_with_meridians: <@{comms_liaison_id}> — "
                    f"status page update due (30-min window). "
                    f"Update statuspage.io now."
                )
            )
            last_external = now
Only the IC or Comms Liaison speaks externally — never individual responders
Post a declaration message internally at T+0 and to the status page by T+30 for SEV1
Use structured update messages every 10 minutes internally and 30 minutes externally, on schedule
Templates for each phase: declaration, update, mitigation, resolution
Enterprise customers get personalised email notifications at T+30, not just a status page link
Safety incidents require Legal review before any external mention of user data or breach scope
GDPR data breaches require DPA notification within 72 hours of becoming aware
Never speculate on root cause, promise resolution times, or use internal jargon in external updates
Automate the comms cadence with a bot reminder so updates are never missed during debugging
